title: VOS develop/7 returns string instead of URI with ORDER BY
description:
With the latest develop/7 sources (commit 8dc86c4) and under OS/X 10.6.8, string literals, instead of URIs, are returned from SPARQL queries. The query:
SELECT * FROM <http://www.openlinksw.com/schemas/virtrdf#>
WHERE { ?s a ?t }

correctly returns rows in the form:
result: [s=uri<http://www.openlinksw.com/virtrdf-data-formats#sql-varchar-literal-nullable>, t=uri<http://www.openlinksw.com/schemas/virtrdf#QuadMapFormat>]

However adding ORDER BY ?s, so the query becomes:
SELECT * FROM <http://www.openlinksw.com/schemas/virtrdf#>
WHERE { ?s a ?t }
ORDER BY ?s

returns the subject as a string:
result: [s=string("http://www.openlinksw.com/virtrdf-data-formats#sql-varchar-literal-nullable"), t=uri<http://www.openlinksw.com/schemas/virtrdf#QuadMapFormat>]

All is well if ORDER BY is followed by LIMIT.
key_bug_features: ORDER BY causes URIs to be returned as strings
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, uri_col URI, string_col VARCHAR(255), nullable_col VARCHAR(255) NULL);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, uri_col, string_col, nullable_col) VALUES (1, uri<http://example.com/resource1>, 'string_value', NULL);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, uri_col, string_col, nullable_col) VALUES (2, uri<http://example.com/resource2>, 'another_string', 'nullable_value');
SELECT * FROM test_data WHERE string_col IS NOT NULL ORDER BY string_col;
SELECT * FROM test_data WHERE uri_col IS NOT NULL ORDER BY uri_col;
